meta.ui_subtitle: Theory exam · sea navigation up to 6 miles from a shelter
meta.ui_demo: Question bank in progress. Questions derived from freely-reusable French law (RIPAM, IALA region A buoyage, Division 240, the 28 Sept. 2007 order). Not an official exam; the bank keeps growing.
meta.ui_sourcenote: Source: French law (official act, freely reusable under the Open Licence / Etalab) — RIPAM, RGP, the 28 September 2007 order, Division 240. No question taken from an operator's bank.

questions.stem: Coming from seaward (IALA region A), what colour and shape is a PORT-hand lateral mark?

provenance.ref: Balisage IALA région A — marques latérales
provenance.source: Balisage maritime — système IALA région A (référentiel permis plaisance)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: IALA region A: the port mark is red and can-shaped; keep it on your left when entering.

questions.stem: Coming from seaward (region A), what colour and shape is a STARBOARD-hand lateral mark?

provenance.ref: Balisage IALA région A — marques latérales
provenance.source: Balisage maritime — système IALA région A (référentiel permis plaisance)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: IALA region A: the starboard mark is green and conical; keep it on your right when entering.

questions.stem: How is a NORTH cardinal mark's topmark recognised, and which side do you pass?

choices.text: Two cones pointing up; pass to the north
Two cones pointing down; pass to the south
Two cones point-to-point; pass to the west

provenance.source: Balisage maritime — système IALA région A (référentiel permis plaisance)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: North cardinal: two black cones, points UP; safe water is to the north, so pass to the north of the mark.

questions.stem: What does a black mark with red horizontal bands, topped by two black spheres, indicate?

choices.text: An isolated danger surrounded by safe water

provenance.ref: Balisage IALA — marque de danger isolé
provenance.source: Balisage maritime — système IALA région A (référentiel permis plaisance)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: It is an isolated-danger mark: moored ON a small danger surrounded by safe water; white light, group flashing (2).

questions.stem: What does a mark with red and white vertical stripes and a red spherical topmark indicate?

choices.text: Safe water all around; pass on either side
The starboard side of a channel

provenance.source: Balisage maritime — système IALA région A (référentiel permis plaisance)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Safe-water mark (mid-channel / landfall): you may pass it on either side.

questions.stem: Two power-driven vessels meet on directly opposite (head-on) courses with risk of collision. What must each do?

choices.text: The faster one keeps clear, the other holds course

provenance.ref: RIPAM règle 14 — Navires faisant des routes opposées
provenance.source: RIPAM — Règlement international pour prévenir les abordages en mer (COLREG 1972)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: COLREG rule 14: each alters course to STARBOARD so they pass port to port.

questions.stem: Two power-driven vessels are on crossing courses with risk of collision. Which one must keep out of the way?

choices.text: The one that has the other on its starboard side
The one that has the other on its port side
The larger of the two vessels

provenance.ref: RIPAM règle 15 — Routes qui se croisent
provenance.source: RIPAM — Règlement international pour prévenir les abordages en mer (COLREG 1972)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: COLREG rule 15: the vessel that has the other on its OWN starboard side gives way; the other (stand-on) keeps course and speed.

questions.stem: One vessel is overtaking another. Which must keep out of the way?

provenance.ref: RIPAM règle 13 — Navire qui en rattrape un autre
provenance.source: RIPAM — Règlement international pour prévenir les abordages en mer (COLREG 1972)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: COLREG rule 13: the OVERTAKING vessel keeps clear of the one being overtaken, whatever the type of either vessel.

questions.stem: Not overtaking and outside a narrow channel, a power-driven vessel meets a vessel under sail. Which one keeps clear?

provenance.source: RIPAM — Règlement international pour prévenir les abordages en mer (COLREG 1972)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: COLREG rule 18: the power-driven vessel keeps out of the way of the sailing vessel.

questions.stem: You are the stand-on vessel that must keep course and speed, but the other vessel takes no action and collision becomes imminent. What may you do?

choices.text: Manoeuvre to avoid the collision yourself
Hold course and speed no matter what

provenance.source: RIPAM — Règlement international pour prévenir les abordages en mer (COLREG 1972)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: COLREG rule 17: if the give-way vessel fails to act, the stand-on vessel may, then must, manoeuvre to avoid collision on its own.

questions.stem: At night, an underway power-driven vessel under 50 m shows sidelights. What colour is the PORT sidelight?

provenance.ref: RIPAM règle 23 — Feux d'un navire à moteur
provenance.source: RIPAM — Règlement international pour prévenir les abordages en mer (COLREG 1972)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: COLREG: port sidelight RED, starboard green; plus a white masthead light and a white sternlight.

questions.stem: In sight of another vessel, a power-driven vessel sounds ONE short blast. What is it signalling?

provenance.ref: RIPAM règle 34 — Signaux de manœuvre (1 son bref)
provenance.source: RIPAM — Règlement international pour prévenir les abordages en mer (COLREG 1972)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: COLREG rule 34: one short blast = “I am altering my course to starboard”.

questions.stem: What do THREE short blasts from a power-driven vessel in sight of another mean?

provenance.ref: RIPAM règle 34 — Signaux de manœuvre (3 sons brefs)
provenance.source: RIPAM — Règlement international pour prévenir les abordages en mer (COLREG 1972)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: COLREG rule 34: three short blasts = “I am operating astern propulsion”.

questions.stem: What do at least FIVE short and rapid blasts mean?

choices.text: Doubt / a warning about the other's manoeuvre

provenance.source: RIPAM — Règlement international pour prévenir les abordages en mer (COLREG 1972)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: COLREG rule 34(d): at least five short rapid blasts signal doubt about the other vessel's intentions or manoeuvre (a warning).

questions.stem: In restricted visibility (fog), what signal does a power-driven vessel underway and making way sound?

choices.text: One prolonged blast at least every 2 minutes

provenance.ref: RIPAM règle 35 — Signaux par visibilité réduite
provenance.source: RIPAM — Règlement international pour prévenir les abordages en mer (COLREG 1972)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: COLREG rule 35: one prolonged blast at intervals of no more than 2 minutes.

questions.stem: Which of these is a recognised PYROTECHNIC distress signal at sea?

provenance.source: Division 240 — matériel de sécurité des navires de plaisance de moins de 24 mètres
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: A red parachute rocket flare is a recognised distress signal; orange smoke also serves by day.

questions.stem: Up to what distance from a shelter does the côtière option of the permis plaisance authorise navigation?

provenance.source: Arrêté du 28 septembre 2007 relatif au permis de conduire des bateaux de plaisance à moteur (référentiel)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: The côtière option allows navigation up to 6 nautical miles from a shelter, day and night, regardless of engine power.

questions.stem: For coastal navigation (up to 6 miles from a shelter), what minimum buoyancy must the personal flotation device have under Division 240?

provenance.source: Division 240 — matériel de sécurité des navires de plaisance de moins de 24 mètres
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Division 240: at least 100 newtons for the coastal zone (beyond 2 and up to 6 miles); 50 N inshore, 150 N offshore.

questions.stem: Which navigation programme does design category “C” of a pleasure boat correspond to?

provenance.source: Division 240 — matériel de sécurité des navires de plaisance de moins de 24 mètres
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Category C = “inshore”: designed for winds up to force 6 and waves up to 2 m. (A = ocean, B = offshore, D = sheltered waters.)

questions.stem: How many people may legally be carried aboard a pleasure boat?

choices.text: The maximum set by the builder
As many as there are lifejackets aboard

provenance.source: Division 240 — matériel de sécurité des navires de plaisance de moins de 24 mètres
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: At most the number set by the builder, shown on the boat's builder's plate / design category.

questions.stem: On a nautical chart, relative to what level are the depths (soundings) given?

provenance.source: Arrêté du 28 septembre 2007 relatif au permis de conduire des bateaux de plaisance à moteur (référentiel)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Soundings are referred to chart datum, close to the level of the lowest tides: actual depth is usually greater.

questions.stem: What is the “tidal range” (marnage)?

choices.text: The height difference between high and low water
The speed of the tidal stream

provenance.source: Arrêté du 28 septembre 2007 relatif au permis de conduire des bateaux de plaisance à moteur (référentiel)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: The tidal range is the height difference between a high water and the following (or preceding) low water.

questions.stem: Between what values does the tidal coefficient vary?

provenance.source: Arrêté du 28 septembre 2007 relatif au permis de conduire des bateaux de plaisance à moteur (référentiel)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: The tidal coefficient ranges from 20 (very weak neap) to 120 (very strong spring); 95 marks the threshold of large tides.

questions.stem: The Beaufort scale, measuring wind force, is graduated from 0 to what value?

provenance.source: Arrêté du 28 septembre 2007 relatif au permis de conduire des bateaux de plaisance à moteur (référentiel)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: The Beaufort scale runs from force 0 (calm) to force 12 (hurricane).

questions.stem: What is the “rule of twelfths” used for?

choices.text: To estimate the water height at a given hour of the tide
To measure drift from the current

provenance.source: Arrêté du 28 septembre 2007 relatif au permis de conduire des bateaux de plaisance à moteur (référentiel)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: It estimates the height of water at a given time between low and high water (1/12, 2/12, 3/12, 3/12, 2/12, 1/12 per hour).

questions.stem: Which VHF channel is reserved for distress, urgency and safety watch, and for calling?

provenance.source: Arrêté du 28 septembre 2007 relatif au permis de conduire des bateaux de plaisance à moteur (référentiel)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Channel 16 is the international distress, urgency, safety and calling channel.

questions.stem: A radio message beginning with “MAYDAY” corresponds to which priority level?

provenance.source: Arrêté du 28 septembre 2007 relatif au permis de conduire des bateaux de plaisance à moteur (référentiel)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: “MAYDAY” = distress: grave and imminent danger, immediate help. “PAN PAN” = urgency; “SÉCURITÉ” = safety.

questions.stem: Above what engine power is the permis plaisance required?

provenance.source: Décret n° 2007-1167 du 2 août 2007 relatif au permis de conduire des bateaux de plaisance à moteur
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: The licence is required to drive a motor pleasure boat whose power exceeds 4.5 kW (about 6 hp).

questions.stem: From what age can the permis plaisance (côtière option) be obtained?

provenance.source: Décret n° 2007-1167 du 2 août 2007 relatif au permis de conduire des bateaux de plaisance à moteur
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: The minimum age to obtain the permis plaisance is 16.

questions.stem: At sea, what does the regulation say about discharging rubbish and hydrocarbons?

provenance.source: Arrêté du 28 septembre 2007 relatif au permis de conduire des bateaux de plaisance à moteur (référentiel)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Discharging rubbish and hydrocarbons into the sea is prohibited; waste must be brought back ashore.

questions.stem: What should the boater do with waste produced aboard?

choices.text: Bring it ashore to harbour facilities

provenance.source: Arrêté du 28 septembre 2007 relatif au permis de conduire des bateaux de plaisance à moteur (référentiel)
provenance.licence: Licence Ouverte / Open Licence 2.0 (Etalab) — French official act, freely reusable with attribution.

questions.explanation: Keep it aboard and dispose of it ashore in the harbour's collection facilities.
